title: Ransomware Deployment — Finance Servers
description: Threat actor compromised VPN gateway via credential stuffing, moved laterally to finance segment, deployed Conti ransomware encrypting 847 files across 3 servers. Detected by behavioral AI 40 minutes into encryption phase.
resolution: Isolated affected servers, restored from immutable backups. Total downtime 6 hours. No ransom paid.
lessons: VPN MFA was not enforced. Implemented mandatory MFA within 24 hours of incident. Deployed honeypot files for early ransomware detection.

title: Credential Stuffing — Customer Portal
description: Automated credential stuffing campaign using 2.3M leaked credential pairs against customer login portal. 847 valid logins identified from new geolocations. Attackers enumerated account balances before detection.
resolution: Rate limiting deployed, affected accounts force-reset, fraud team notified for 847 accounts.
lessons: Login anomaly detection was insufficient. Deployed impossible travel detection and device fingerprinting.

title: Insider Threat — Bulk Data Exfiltration
description: Departing employee downloaded 14GB of customer PII data over 3-day period prior to resignation. Exfiltrated via personal Dropbox account. DLP alert triggered on day 3.
resolution: HR notified, employee access revoked, legal hold placed, customer notifications sent.
lessons: DLP rules were not tuned to detect gradual exfiltration. Implemented user behavior analytics with 30-day lookback on departing employees.

title: Log4Shell Exploitation — Apache Server
description: Automated scanner exploited CVE-2021-44228 on public-facing Apache server. Attacker established reverse shell and began internal network reconnaissance before WAF rule deployed.
resolution: Server isolated, patch applied, WAF rule deployed across all public services within 2 hours.
lessons: Vulnerability was 3 days old at time of exploit. Implemented 24-hour patch SLA for CVSS 10 vulnerabilities.

title: Phishing — CFO Credential Compromise
description: Targeted spear phishing email impersonating IT helpdesk prompted CFO to enter credentials on cloned login page. Attacker accessed email and finance approval workflows before detection via impossible travel alert.
resolution: Account suspended, password reset, email audit completed, suspicious approvals reversed.
lessons: Finance executives need phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2). C-suite enrolled in hardware security key program.

title: SSH Brute Force — Jump Server
description: Sustained brute force attack against internet-facing jump server over 18 hours. 48,000 authentication attempts from 12 distributed IPs. No successful compromise — account lockout triggered after 5 failures.
resolution: IPs blocked at perimeter firewall. No breach confirmed.
lessons: Jump server should not be internet-facing. Migrated to VPN-gated access model.

title: Crypto Mining Malware — Dev Server
description: Developer server compromised via exposed Docker API socket. Attacker deployed XMRig miner consuming 95% CPU. Detected via performance monitoring alert after 6 hours.
resolution: Container killed, Docker API access restricted, server reimaged.
lessons: Development infrastructure had insufficient network segmentation. Docker sockets now require TLS client certificates.

title: False Positive — Pen Test Without Notice
description: Multiple CRITICAL alerts triggered including port scanning, vulnerability exploitation, and lateral movement. Investigated for 45 minutes before discovering authorized penetration test was in progress without proper SOC notification.
resolution: Alerts acknowledged, pen test scope whitelisted for duration.
lessons: Penetration testing calendar must be shared with SOC in advance. Created mandatory pre-test notification process.

title: Supply Chain Alert — Suspicious Update
description: Threat intelligence feed alerted on npm package used in CI/CD pipeline being flagged for typosquat. Package was lodash-utils vs legitimate lodash. Removed before any production deployment.
resolution: Malicious package removed, all developer machines scanned, CI/CD pipeline audited.
lessons: Dependency scanning added to CI/CD pipeline. Private npm registry enforced.

title: DDoS Attack — Customer API Gateway
description: Layer 7 HTTP flood targeting /api/search endpoint at 280,000 req/sec for 45 minutes. API gateway rate limiting triggered after 3 minutes. Partial service degradation during peak attack.
resolution: CloudFlare Under Attack Mode activated, rate limits tuned, affected region traffic rerouted.
lessons: API endpoint lacked request costing. Implemented query complexity limits and CDN WAF rules.

title: Lateral Movement — Post-VPN Breach
description: Attacker with valid VPN credentials (obtained via phishing) performed systematic internal reconnaissance over 8 hours. Attempted to access 23 internal hosts using default credentials. Two workstations accessed before isolation.
resolution: VPN access revoked, affected workstations isolated and reimaged, all default credentials changed.
lessons: East-west traffic monitoring was insufficient. Deployed micro-segmentation and network behavioral analysis.

title: Web Shell Deployment — Legacy CMS
description: Attacker exploited CVE-2023-XXXX in legacy CMS to upload PHP web shell. Shell used for reconnaissance and file system browsing for 12 hours. No data exfiltration confirmed.
resolution: Web shell removed, CMS updated, FIM alerts deployed, public-facing CMS migrated to managed service.
lessons: Legacy CMS was 3 major versions behind. Implemented quarterly vulnerability assessment for all public services.

title: Kerberoasting Attack — Service Accounts
description: Attacker with low-privilege domain account requested TGS tickets for 15 service accounts with SPNs. Offline cracking of 3 service account passwords successful. No further use before detection.
resolution: Service account passwords reset, privileged accounts migrated to gMSA, auditing enabled for TGS requests.
lessons: Service accounts used weak passwords (under 20 chars). Implemented 30-char minimum for service accounts.

title: Email BEC — Finance Wire Transfer Attempt
description: Business email compromise attempt impersonating CEO to request $240,000 wire transfer. Email passed SPF/DKIM due to look-alike domain. Finance team escalated per protocol before executing.
resolution: Wire transfer prevented, look-alike domain reported and taken down, DMARC enforcement tightened.
lessons: DMARC policy was in monitoring mode only. Changed to p=reject. Added wire transfer callback verification requirement.

title: Cloud IAM Privilege Escalation
description: Developer API key leaked in public GitHub repository. Attacker used key to enumerate AWS resources and escalate privileges via misconfigured IAM role. Created backdoor IAM user before detection.
resolution: Compromised key revoked, backdoor user removed, all IAM policies audited, GitGuardian deployed.
lessons: No secret scanning in CI/CD pre-commit hooks. Deployed git-secrets and HashiCorp Vault for all credentials.
